questions.question: Why is coherent detection preferred over non-coherent detection in PSK systems under AWGN?

answers.b: It does not require carrier synchronization.
answers.c: It provides better bit error performance.

explanations.a: Incorrect! Coherent detection is usually more complex.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Coherent detection requires carrier synchronization.
explanations.c: Correct! Coherent detection provides superior BER performance due to phase alignment.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Power consumption is not the main factor in choosing coherent detection.

questions.question: Consider a binary PSK system. What is the minimum Euclidean distance between symbols if the signal power is normalized to 1?

explanations.a: Correct! In BPSK, symbols are +1 and −1, so the Euclidean distance between them is 2.
explanations.b: Incorrect! This is smaller than the actual distance between BPSK symbols.
explanations.c: Incorrect! This value corresponds to QPSK, not BPSK.
explanations.d: Incorrect! This is not the correct Euclidean distance.

questions.question: In digital modulation, what factor primarily determines the bandwidth efficiency of a scheme?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Symbol rate affects bandwidth, but efficiency depends more on how much information each symbol carries.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Modulation index is mainly relevant for analog modulation like FM.
explanations.c: Correct! Bandwidth efficiency increases with the number of bits per symbol (log₂M), though pulse shaping also plays a role.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Carrier frequency does not determine bandwidth efficiency.

questions.question: In BPSK, what is the theoretical bit error rate (BER) under AWGN with coherent detection and Eb/N0 = 10 dB?

explanations.a: Incorrect! BPSK BER follows Q(√(2Eb/N0)), not Q(√(Eb/N0)).
explanations.b: Correct! BPSK BER ≈ Q(√(2Eb/N0)), and for Eb/N0 = 10 (linear), this gives √20.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Although related, this is not the standard Q-function expression used here.
explanations.d: Incorrect! This does not match the correct BPSK BER formula.

questions.question: In M-ary PSK, what is the primary trade-off when increasing the value of M?

answers.a: Lower data rate but better noise immunity
answers.b: Higher bandwidth requirement and lower complexity
answers.c: Higher spectral efficiency but increased symbol error rate
answers.d: Increased power efficiency but reduced robustness

explanations.a: Incorrect! Increasing M increases data rate.
explanations.b: Incorrect! Bandwidth efficiency increases with M, but complexity also increases.
explanations.c: Correct! Larger M increases bits per symbol but reduces the distance between symbols, increasing symbol error rate.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Power efficiency does not necessarily improve with larger M.

questions.question: How does Gray coding benefit digital modulation schemes?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Gray coding does not directly affect signal power.
explanations.b: Correct! Adjacent symbols differ by only one bit, reducing bit errors.
explanations.c: Incorrect! Bandwidth is not affected by Gray coding.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Gray coding does not directly improve synchronization.

questions.question: For a signal with bit rate R and modulation with k bits per symbol, what is the symbol rate?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Addition is dimensionally inconsistent and does not represent symbol rate.
explanations.b: Correct! Symbol rate = Bit rate / Bits per symbol (R/k).
explanations.c: Incorrect! Multiplying would incorrectly increase the rate.
explanations.d: Incorrect! This is not a valid relationship for symbol rate.

questions.question: Which digital modulation scheme is most robust to noise in an AWGN channel?

explanations.a: Incorrect! ASK is highly sensitive to amplitude noise.
explanations.b: Incorrect! FSK is more robust than ASK but generally less efficient than PSK.
explanations.c: Correct! PSK, especially BPSK, offers the best noise performance in AWGN among these schemes.
explanations.d: Incorrect! Different modulation schemes have different noise performance.

questions.question: A digital system transmits 16-QAM symbols at 1 Msymbol/s. What is the bit rate of the system?

explanations.a: Incorrect! Each 16-QAM symbol carries 4 bits, not 1 bit.
explanations.c: Correct! 16-QAM = log2(16) = 4 bits per symbol → 1M × 4 = 4 Mbps.
explanations.d: Incorrect! 16-QAM does not carry 16 bits per symbol.

questions.question: In a binary ASK system with coherent detection, what kind of filter maximizes the SNR at the detector?

explanations.a: Incorrect! A low-pass filter alone does not maximize SNR.
explanations.b: Correct! A matched filter maximizes the signal-to-noise ratio in AWGN for coherent detection.
explanations.c: Incorrect! A band-pass filter does not provide optimal SNR performance.
explanations.d: Incorrect! This is used in envelope detection, not optimal coherent detection.
